title: Dendrophorbium krukoffii
intro: Dendrophorbium krukoffii, also known as the Krukoff's costus root, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in the treatment of various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of D. krukoffii include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, D. krukoffii is commonly used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and arthritis.
taxonomy: Dendrophorbium krukoffii is classified within the family Asteraceae and the genus Dendrophorbium. The genus Dendrophorbium is a part of the tribe Eupatorieae. This classification suggests that Dendrophorbium krukoffii is a member of the aster or sunflower family. The tribe Eupatorieae is known to comprise flowering plants with various life forms, including shrubs and trees. Dendrophorbium krukoffii is native to Central and South America.

The plant is also used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
